decisions.decision: v7.0 complete rewrite instead of patching v6.0
decisions.why: v5→v6 had 6 hotfixes in 50 mins; patching mobile issues was slower than rewriting with mobile-first approach

decisions.decision: Replace 11 learning systems with single PROJECT.md + NW-MEMORY
decisions.why: Old systems added 374KB dead weight, none actually learned; compounding comes from git-derived patterns not static docs

decisions.decision: Use NW_I18N.register per page, not a centralized translation file
decisions.why: Per-page keeps translations close to code; centralized file would be huge and cause merge conflicts

decisions.decision: setTimeout(0) trick for HTML content after NW_I18N applies textContent
decisions.why: Core applies textContent first, then onChange fires — setTimeout defers innerHTML write to next tick

decisions.decision: Separate tabletop-stats.json from cards-v2.json
decisions.why: Digital rebalancing must never break D&D stats; separate files = separate balance cycles

decisions.decision: Generate D&D stats from flavorStats seed, not gameStats
decisions.why: flavorStats.power/endurance are decorative — perfect seed for STR/CON without coupling to battle balance

decisions.decision: Star level unlocks tabletop abilities, not stat boosts
decisions.why: Stat boosts are boring in D&D; unique abilities per star create real progression + Sacred Log sink

decisions.decision: Star abilities are Sacred Log sinks at 50/100/200/350/500 per level
decisions.why: Doubles currency drain without new currencies; gives duplicates purpose in both digital and tabletop

decisions.decision: Shrine cards derive flavorStats from gameStats at 90% power
decisions.why: Shrine cards had empty flavorStats; using gameStats*0.9 preserves underdog design in tabletop

decisions.decision: Progressive disclosure via nav filtering, not a new system
decisions.why: Filter existing nav sections based on NW_WALLET stats; no new localStorage keys, no new modules, all pages still accessible by direct URL

decisions.decision: Progressive disclosure: filter nav sections by player tier (0-5) based on wallet stats
decisions.why: 80-page nav overwhelms new players; Day 1 should show 6 pages max; existing players auto-detect; all pages accessible by direct URL

decisions.decision: Welcome Pack: 3 free Sacred Logs on wallet creation
decisions.why: 7-day wall to first pull killed Day 1 retention; 3 logs = first multi-pull experience in first 2 minutes

decisions.decision: Battle gives Gold + 10% Sacred Log chance per win with streak bonuses
decisions.why: Battle had zero rewards; players had no reason to replay; now it drives the core loop

decisions.decision: Daily login gives Sacred Logs on Day 3, Day 5, Day 7 (not just Day 7)
decisions.why: Old: 1 log per week. New: 5 logs per week from daily alone. Matches target of ~1 pull/day for active F2P

decisions.decision: Hero CTA links to /forge and /battle instead of scrolling to lore sections
decisions.why: Homepage had zero links to game actions; new player had no CTA to start playing

decisions.decision: Strip emojis with text-only cleanup (no SVG injection into 4329 lines)
decisions.why: 4329 emoji lines across 152 files — injecting SVG spans would bloat HTML 3-5x and break text nodes in translations. Stripping is clean, reversible, and NW_ICONS handles the few UI icons that need SVGs.

decisions.decision: v5 regex uses only single-char trailing space removal after emoji, no multi-char cleanup patterns
decisions.why: v3/v4 used aggressive post-strip patterns that matched HTML attributes. v5 only removes emoji+optional-trailing-space. Safe for all HTML contexts.

decisions.decision: Auto-compact in pre-commit hook when memory.json > 200KB
decisions.why: 975KB memory.json with 9196 dead coChange pairs from deleted files. Without auto-compact, every commit grows the file with stale data. 200KB limit keeps it under control permanently.

decisions.decision: Filter archive/, .husky/, dist/, memory.json from query display and pattern tracking
decisions.why: These paths are noise — archive is old, .husky changes are config, dist is generated. Showing them in hotspots/coChanges wastes context window for AI sessions.

decisions.decision: Strip [ZH]/[TH] placeholder prefixes instead of adding real translations
decisions.why: 3792 placeholders across 69 pages. Real translation of all strings is a massive effort. Stripping prefixes shows English as fallback, which is functional. Real zh/th translations can be added incrementally per-page.

decisions.decision: Rewrite sentinel i18n module to check [ZH]/[TH] placeholders, missing keys, and dual i18n systems
decisions.why: Old module said 83/B+ when 3792 strings were untranslated. New module correctly shows 15/F. Sentinel must tell the truth.

decisions.decision: Move snapshot from pre-commit to post-commit hook
decisions.why: Pre-commit runs BEFORE commit exists, so git rev-parse HEAD returns the previous commit hash. Post-commit has the real hash. memory.json is staged by the next commit's pre-commit hook — always one commit behind in git, but always current on disk for --query.

decisions.decision: Pre-commit: auto-compact + stage memory.json only. Post-commit: full snapshot.
decisions.why: Separation of concerns. Pre-commit is for preparation (clean/stage). Post-commit is for recording (snapshot). No infinite loops, no race conditions.

decisions.decision: Hearthstone-style contextual coach for first battle — just-in-time tooltips, not upfront reading
decisions.why: Research: Hearthstone scripted tutorial limits actions to correct ones + shows one concept per battle. Marvel Snap first-time UX review highlights contextual hints. ACM CHI 2022 paper on 'just-in-time reminders' pattern. Our implementation: 10 steps, MutationObserver-driven, auto-advances when player does the right thing, localStorage flag prevents re-show, SKIP TUTORIAL button always visible.

decisions.decision: Coach is a separate file (nw-battle-coach.js) — does not touch nw-battle-v7.js
decisions.why: Battle v7 has 5 fix chains in recent history. Touching it risks breakage. Coach uses MutationObservers and event listeners to detect game state without modifying battle code. If coach breaks, battle still works perfectly.

decisions.decision: Smart Glow + Auto-Target + Auto-Play as separate file nw-battle-assist.js
decisions.why: Keeps battle engine untouched; coach pattern proven; reads via NW_BATTLE API; skips visuals when coach is active

decisions.decision: Attacker scoring: scoreAttacker() picks best board card to attack with; shows ATK tag on board
decisions.why: Reduces decision paralysis — player just taps the green-glowing card

decisions.decision: Use the conversation AI as the LLM-as-Judge instead of an external API
decisions.why: No free LLM API available. The AI assistant already running in the conversation IS the judge — it reads .nw-context, self-evaluates before committing, and records learnings. Pre-commit hook does deterministic constraint checking. No API needed.

decisions.decision: Blueprint concepts adapted for no-API environment
decisions.why: From the Recursive Self-Improvement blueprint: 1) Commit scoring (deterministic, 0-100) replaces rubric-based LLM scoring. 2) Auto-reflection on fix chains replaces LLM critique. 3) Semantic distillation from hotspots/coChanges replaces graph DB. 4) Pre-commit constraint checker replaces LLM-as-Judge. 5) .nw-context file replaces vector DB retrieval — always fresh on disk.

decisions.decision: NumbahWan as primary font everywhere on homepage
decisions.why: User wants the custom pixel font to be THE identity of the site, not just headings — Orbitron is fallback only

decisions.decision: Built /oracle page as a life-advice generator combining Buddhist sutras, Tao Te Ching, Bible, and Quran with humor — fallback pool of 20 deep wisdoms so it works even without LLM API
decisions.why: User wants an addictive wisdom page that feels like a premium product. Fallback pool makes it usable immediately without waiting for API key to be configured on Cloudflare.

decisions.decision: Oracle page uses keyword-matched curated wisdom pool (20 entries across 15 life topics) as primary content source, with LLM as enhancement when API key is valid
decisions.why: API tokens expire between sessions — curated pool must be rich enough to stand alone as the full product. Each wisdom follows the 5-part Oracle format: empathy, consensus, funny truth, action step, seal.

decisions.decision: Enriched deepReflect with 6 organic analysis types: decision-impact correlation, unprotected area detection, recurring theme analysis, velocity/sprint quality, score trend analysis, focus shift detection — all derived from real accumulated data, no fake entries
decisions.why: Learnings and reflections arrays were empty or contained only template fix-chain messages. The system had rich data (32 decisions, 12 breakages, 101 snapshots) but wasn't synthesizing cross-cutting insights from it.

decisions.decision: viral-ready README + tools page + CONTRIBUTING + issue templates
decisions.why: GitHub repos that go viral have: badges, copy-paste commands, visual terminal demos, contributor CTA, issue templates

ios.fact: touchend + click both fire on iOS — use handling flag with 300ms timeout
Audio.play() blocked until user gesture — never autoplay, init on first tap
Safari blocks Audio context creation outside user gesture — wrap in click/touchstart handler
history.back() unreliable in SPA-like setup — use direct window.location.href

cloudflare.fact: Workers have 10ms CPU limit (free) / 50ms (paid) — no heavy computation server-side
D1 is SQLite — no ALTER COLUMN, no concurrent writes, 1MB response limit
Static assets in public/ copied to dist/ by Vite — changes need rebuild

i18n.fact: All 3 languages (EN/ZH/TH) required in every NW_I18N.register call
data-i18n uses textContent (strips HTML) — use data-i18n-html for content with tags
NW_I18N.onChange callback fires AFTER core applies textContent — use setTimeout(0) to override with innerHTML
nw_lang localStorage key — never use a different key for language storage
Never generate [ZH]/[TH] placeholder prefixes — use English fallback directly. Prefixes are visible to users and look broken.

battle.fact: Mobile viewport must handle 320-375px — test at minimum 320px
Card hand must use flex-wrap or scroll — overflow:hidden clips cards on mobile
Battle must always give rewards — never zero. Even a loss gives 10 Gold consolation.
nw-battle-coach.js must load AFTER nw-battle-v7.js — it hooks into DOM elements created by battle init
nw-battle-assist.js must load AFTER nw-battle-v7.js; it reads window.NW_BATTLE API which is set at IIFE end
Assist skips all visuals when coach overlay is active (coachActive() checks for #coachOverlay)

dom.fact: innerHTML replaces DOM and destroys event handlers — rebind after innerHTML updates

modules.fact: NW_* modules init via nw-boot.js load order — dependency order matters
One localStorage writer per key — avoid two modules writing same key

tabletop.fact: Never import tabletop-stats.json into digital battle — one-way reference only
D&D CR must respect rarity: Common CR1-2, Uncommon CR3-4, Rare CR5-7, Epic CR8-10, Legendary CR11-14, Mythic CR15+
Physical card back will print tabletop stats — keep data structure print-friendly

nav.fact: All pages must remain accessible by direct URL even if hidden from nav — never block access, only hide discovery
Existing players with stats > 0 should see everything unlocked immediately on first load — no regression
Existing players with stats > 0 must see everything unlocked immediately on first load — no regression

economy.fact: F2P target: ~1 Sacred Log per day for active players (battle + daily + arcade). Never below this.
Sacred Logs cannot be purchased directly — maintain prestige; only earned through gameplay

emoji.fact: Emoji regex must cover U+2B50 (star), U+200D (ZWJ), U+FE0F (variation selector), and U+20E3 (keycap) — these are outside the common ranges

regex.fact: Never include quote characters in post-strip cleanup regex patterns — they match HTML attribute closing quotes and corrupt tag attributes

workflow.fact: core.hooksPath=.husky — .git/hooks/ are ignored. All hook changes must go in .husky/ to be portable.
memory.json is always one commit behind in git but current on disk — post-commit writes it, next pre-commit stages it
Always run setup_github_environment BEFORE any git fetch/push/pull — token expires between sessions, never try unauthenticated first
When user asks a question, clarify intent BEFORE building — do not assume. Ask what they mean if ambiguous.
Before recommending a feature, grep/read the codebase to check if it already exists — read .nw-context inventory AND the actual files
Pre-commit hook blocks commits without .nw-session file and without at least 1 learning recorded — AI must follow CLAUDE.md startup checklist every session

oracle.fact: The OPENAI_API_KEY in .dev.vars and GENSPARK_TOKEN env var can expire — Oracle API falls back to 20 curated wisdoms gracefully. On Cloudflare production, set OPENAI_API_KEY and OPENAI_BASE_URL as secrets for live LLM responses.
Oracle fallback wisdom must follow the 5-part format: empathy opener, ancient consensus (Buddhism/Taoism/Bible/Quran), funny truth, actionable step, oracle seal. Never generic quotes.

breakages.what: v5.0 used overflow:hidden on arena — clipped hand cards on mobile (320px)
Audio.init() called during page load blocked iOS init — hand appeared empty until tap
Deck building ran at page load — cards dealt before player ready, no visual feedback
debug overlay left in production — forgot to gate behind GM mode check
Auto-start overlay detection failed on iOS — overlay was display:none but still 'visible' in JS check
conspiracy.html had two i18n systems (CONSPIRACY_I18N + NW_I18N) — translations conflicted
lore/conspiracy-board innerHTML content lost on language switch — NW_I18N.onChange textContent overwrote HTML
nw-guide.js event handlers lost after language switch — innerHTML replaced DOM nodes
v4 emoji-strip regex cleanup pattern matched closing HTML attribute quotes — collapsed attribute sequences (e.g. data-x="y" class="z" became d). Never include quote chars in post-strip cleanup regex.
3792 [ZH]/[TH] placeholder strings visible to users across 69 pages — generated by i18n-auto-fix.cjs which should never prefix translations with language markers
AI rewrote loading screen for 20 minutes when user was asking about the recursive learning system — did not clarify intent
AI recommended auto-premortem feature that already existed in .husky/pre-commit — did not check before suggesting

battle has 5 recorded decisions but still 5 breakages — decisions alone aren't preventing bugs here. The area needs structural protection (tests, guards, or simplification), not just documented intent.
i18n has 3 recorded decisions but still 3 breakages — decisions alone aren't preventing bugs here. The area needs structural protection (tests, guards, or simplification), not just documented intent.

workflow has 2 recorded decisions but still 2 breakages — decisions alone aren't preventing bugs here. The area needs structural protection (tests, guards, or simplification), not just documented intent.
guide has 1 breakage(s) but zero documented decisions — it's a blind spot. Before touching guide, record why things are the way they are.
"innerHTML/DOM" appears in 2 separate breakages — this is a systemic weakness, not isolated incidents. Every change in this area needs the same pre-check.
"iOS/mobile" appears in 3 separate breakages — this is a systemic weakness, not isolated incidents. Every change in this area needs the same pre-check.
"language/i18n" appears in 4 separate breakages — this is a systemic weakness, not isolated incidents. Every change in this area needs the same pre-check.
"AI workflow" appears in 2 separate breakages — this is a systemic weakness, not isolated incidents. Every change in this area needs the same pre-check.
Sprint days average 77/100 vs normal days 68/100 — quality holds under pressure. Current workflow handles high-volume days well.
Commit quality is declining: first half averaged 77/100, recent half 69/100 (-8). Commits are getting less focused or more sprawling.

Project focus has shifted. New focus areas: public/research, public/static/helpers, public/static/templates, public/tabletop, src/data, public/static/fonts, oracle. Moved away from: docs. Check if abandoned areas have unfinished work or growing debt.

learnings.lesson: auto-compact runs inside saveMemory — any function that loads, modifies, then saves memory will trigger compaction mid-flight. New features (like deepReflect) must account for this by running BEFORE saveMemory, not after.
deepReflect must run before saveMemory because auto-compact triggers inside saveMemory

learning system upgrades: --areamap builds area-file map from git history, --whyfile shows full file intelligence, --postfix auto-analyzes fix commits, --learned records fix lessons, enhanced premortem shows recent commits and area files — all zero-dependency, all driven by real git data

chat compaction destroys ephemeral todo lists — the permanent fix is writing WIP state to disk (.nw-wip) and surfacing it in .nw-context. After compaction, AI reads .nw-context, sees WIP section, and resumes instead of re-planning from scratch

elite learning system: 3 features — (1) --guard auto-detects areas from file paths and shows only dangerous constraints/breakages/learnings + coupled-file warnings, replaces inline pre-commit checker; (2) --gen-tests converts breakages into runnable regression tests with block-scoped file checks; (3) --export-shared/--import-shared writes universal constraints to ~/.nw-shared-memory.json for cross-project portability

uber-useful for all builders: (1) classifyArea now auto-detects from .nw-config.json custom areas + universal patterns (tests/, i18n/, auth/, api/, devops/) with legacy NumbahWan fallback; (2) --init does zero-config setup for any project; (3) --onboard explains system to new AI in 10 lines; (4) --status gives one-screen dashboard with health bar, risk areas, hotspots, learnings; (5) noise paths configurable via .nw-config.json
classifyArea must check Object.keys(CONFIG.areas).length > 0, not just truthy CONFIG.areas — empty object {} is truthy but has no entries, skipping legacy patterns
